programme.title: All That Breathes
programme.desc: Amidst the darkening backdrop of Delhi's apocalyptic air and escalating violence, two brothers devote their lives to protect one casualty of the turbulent times - the bird known as the Black Kite. Documentary, starring Salik Rehman, Mohammad Saud and Nadeem Shehzad.

programme.title: On the Line: The Richard Williams Story
programme.desc: Featuring newly uncovered interviews spanning the 1980s to now, Richard Williams - the father of tennis stars Venus and Serena - retraces his family's journey from the poverty-stricken streets of Shreveport, Louisiana, to the grass courts of Wimbledon.

programme.title: My Icon: Heather Watson
programme.desc: British tennis star Heather Watson discusses the individuals who have inspired her over the course of her life.

programme.title: Dogtown and Z-Boys
programme.desc: Documentary charting the history of skateboarding, from its 1970s emergence as a land-based form of surfing in Los Angeles and its subsequent explosion of popularity worldwide, to its current status as a widely-practised extreme sport. Narrated by Sean Penn, with contributions from experts Jay Adams and Tony Alva.

programme.title: My Icon: Shaun Gayle
programme.desc: The former Chicago Bears defensive back and Super Bowl winner discusses the individuals that have inspired him during his life.

programme.title: Hitsville: The Making of Motown
programme.desc: Rare performances, interviews and rarely seen footage come together to paint a picture of the history and origins of the Motown record label.

programme.title: I Am Heath Ledger
programme.desc: Naomi Watts, Ben Mendelsohn, Ang Lee are among the contributors to a documentary celebrating the life and work of the Australian actor, who died in 2008.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: Investigating the unsolved 2015 murder of John Palmer, who was nicknamed Goldfinger after his involvement in melting down gold from the 1983 Brink's-Mat robbery. Palmer was found dead in his Essex garden and while police originally believed he died of natural causes, they eventually confirmed the case was being treated as a professional hit.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: The search for John Palmer's killer leads to Tenerife, where he built a timeshare empire. Undercover stings and Russian mob ties expose the scale of his power.

programme.title: The Essex Murders: Who Killed Goldfinger?
programme.desc: A professional hitman is suspected in John Palmer's murder, with evidence pointing to a cartel and an Eastern European enforcer.

programme.title: The Two Escobars
programme.desc: How the fates of two Colombians - Andrés Escobar, the inspirational captain of Nacional, and Pablo Escobar, the notorious leader of the Medellin cartel - were permanently linked.

programme.title: Pete Doherty: Stranger in My Own Skin
programme.desc: Documentary about the musician and Libertines co-frontman, filmed over the course of 10 years and charting his fight to overcome his demons and fight addiction.

programme.title: Busby
programme.desc: A profile of former Manchester United manager Matt Busby, who transformed football through a career of great talent and tragedy. Featuring previously unseen archive footage and interviews with those who knew the man best.

programme.desc: The life and career of the English actor, known for his roles in Schindler's List, The Grand Budapest Hotel and The Menu.

programme.desc: The life and work of Sidney Lumet, who was Oscar-nominated for his direction of 12 Angry Men, Dog Day Afternoon, Network and The Verdict.

programme.desc: Documentary charting the life and career of the comedy actor, and her lasting influence on the portrayal and roles of women in film and TV.

programme.desc: Documentary about famed tennis coach Nick Bollettieri, who guided the likes of Jim Courier, Monica Seles and Andre Agassi to glory, but his success came at a terrible personal cost.

programme.title: Pantani: The Accidental Death of a Cyclist
programme.desc: Documentary about the rise and fall of Marco Pantani, the maverick Italian cyclist found dead less than six years after winning the Tour de France.

programme.title: My Icon: Linford Christie
programme.desc: The former sprint superstar and Olympic gold medalist discusses the individuals that have inspired him in his life and career.

programme.title: David Holmes: The Boy Who Lived
programme.desc: Documentary telling the story of Daniel Radcliffe's Harry Potter stunt double David Holmes, who was left paralysed from the chest down after an on-set accident in 2009. Featuring personal footage shot over the last decade, behind-the-scenes material from Holmes's stunt work, scenes of his current life and interviews with David as well as Daniel Radcliffe, friends, family and former crewmates.

programme.title: My Icon: Tanni Grey Thompson (Pt1)
programme.desc: The former paralympian discusses the individuals that have inspired her.

programme.title: Bugs That Ruled the World
programme.desc: Exploring how the praying mantis has the agility of a martial artist and an appetite for everything from flies to hummingbirds.

programme.title: Bugs That Ruled the World
programme.desc: How beetles keep ecosystems in balance with biological weapons like tusks and toxic sprays and why they are the world's unsung heroes.

programme.title: The Island of Giants
programme.desc: Exploring how whale sharks drive the culture of Santa Maria, the only European hotspot for these giants and why warming seas may be drawing them in.

programme.title: The Kimberley: Australia's Wild West
programme.desc: A look at Australia's Wild West - The Kimberley - the animals who live there and tracking the quests of those working to protect them.

programme.title: Ireland's Wild Islands with Eoin Warner
programme.desc: The wildlife presenter sails a 140-year-old Galway hooker out into the Atlantic to showcase Ireland's western islands. Eoin starts his island voyage on Rathlin, then heads west for Malin Head and Inishtrahull, seeing basking sharks and dolphins, before going south to Achill Island.

programme.title: Ireland's Wild Islands with Eoin Warner
programme.desc: Eoin visits a native woodland on Clare Island and as he sails through Connemara's waters, he finds deserted islands home to skylarks and Ireland's only native reptile on the Aran Islands.

programme.title: Ireland's Wild Islands with Eoin Warner
programme.desc: Eoin is back in his Galway hooker and sails to the islands off Co Kerry, before heading south to his home waters off Cork and ending his journey at Fastnet Rock.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough employs the latest technologies to explore the violence, rivalries and deadly weaponry existing within the world of bugs. This first episode examines the survival tactics of its terrifying residents including killer ants, trap-setting spiders and beetles with the ability to shoot boiling chemicals at their enemies.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster continues his bug-eyed view of the world of creepy-crawlies, revealing how predators defuse the defences of their prey. Highlights include the cockroach wasp, busy preparing a tasty - and very live - treat for its young, the whirligig beetle, which employs a water-based radar system, and the jumping portia spider, which feeds on other arachnids.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A further selection of creepy-crawly close-ups, focusing on courtship in the bug world. Highlights include scorpions shaking their booty, the perfume-wearing nasonia wasp and the tropical cricket, for whom romance goes out of the window as it uses bribery to seal the deal with would-be partners. One potential partner to steer well clear of, however, is the praying mantis, the female of which is no

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: A close-up view of sex, bug-style, as David Attenborough talks viewers through the different ways in which creepy-crawlies reproduce. Size matters for the minuscule male orb spider, creepily sneaking up on its intended and trying to mate without her noticing, while there's no rest for the lothario-like butterfly, which has plenty of notches on its proverbial bedpost. However, the harvestman spider

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: David Attenborough shifts the focus to bugs that prefer co-operation rather than conflict. They include burrowing cockroaches, the suitably named social spiders - which share a 30-metre web - the queen bee and her happy hive of workers and green ants pulling together to build a nest.

programme.title: Micro Monsters with David Attenborough
programme.desc: The broadcaster concludes his close-up examination of the creepy-crawly world, shifting focus to the bugs' ultimate invention - the colony. Key residents of these minuscule mega-cities include honey bees, termites, leafcutter ants - who have a knack for farming - and their Argentine cousins, members of a community so vast it spans an entire continent.

programme.desc: Inara searches for her kidnapped cubs and Chilli stalks a warthog, while struggling to keep all nine of the young cheetahs fed.

programme.title: Big Cat Country
programme.desc: Documentary about three dynasties of lions, with the MK pride defending the territory they have held for years from the all-male Nomad pride and the all-female Hollywood pride.

programme.title: Supersenses

This documentary series begins by exploring animal vision, a world where dragonflies see in almost every direction, a tiny crustacean boasts hexnocular vision and pigeons see 10 times the colour that humans can.

programme.title: Supersenses
programme.desc: The documentary asks what makes a truly super-powered animal, scouring the globe for the strangest and most surprising animal skills and, in a round of competitive challenges, revealing which creature holds the title for super strength, most toxic, master of disguise, greatest escape artist and master builder.

programme.title: Supersenses
programme.desc: The amazing world of what animals really see with their extraordinary senses, thanks to remarkable CGI and innovative film techniques.

programme.title: Strange Creatures
programme.desc: The weirdest wildlife wonders of the natural world, including a very strange plant, as well as four animals known for their bad toilet etiquette.

programme.title: Strange Creatures
programme.desc: The weirdest-looking birds are often also the most incredible survivors. Check out the winged weirdos whose strange looks keep them alive.

programme.title: Strange Creatures
programme.desc: Some of the most hideous animals in the world owe their survival to their bad looks. They may be grotesque but their evolution is amazing.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The builders battle extreme weather in Sacramento, Ky. to save a cabin built in 1853.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Maine Cabin Masters offer a tour of their home turf and highlight some of their favorite areas, activities and cabins across the Pine Tree State.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Adaptive Outdoor Education Center (AOEC) is looking to use their pond by creating a trail and elevated clubhouse overlooking the water, so the team must apply ADA compliant techniques to ensure that the path and structure are accessible for all.

programme.title: This Old House
programme.desc: At the Russell Woods project, Tommy uses a clever fix for some damaged oak flooring in the living room; Frank and Tamiko meet with Jerusha Kaffine, a local kitchen designer, to see her plan; repairing the front steps before new gutters go up.

programme.title: This Old House
programme.desc: New cabinets and kitchen tiles at the Grandmont Rosedale project; reinstall leaded glass bay windows at Russell Woods; wood grain tile; a decoupling membrane underlayment that supports electric wire heating; urban farming in Detroit; a drywell.

programme.title: This Old House
programme.desc: Frank and Tamiko prep the guest room for painting; Tommy repairs crown molding in the living room; installing plumbing fixtures; Tommy and Frank fit an interior door into an old opening; a fireplace facelift using 100-year-old pottery.

programme.title: This Old House
programme.desc: Kevin and Tommy pull up to the completed Russell Woods project to reflect on their time in the city and restoration efforts; an overview of the furnace, water heater and water filtration system; selling the property; feedback from homebuyers.

programme.title: Building Back America's Trades
programme.desc: Deadlines loom as students and mentors race to build homes, secure jobs, and chart their futures. Doubt, discrimination, and hard choices lie around every corner as the realities of the present interrupt dreams of skilled trades futures.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: Matt and Samantha Hardy look to move from Austin to Waco, Texas, and purchase their first house together.

programme.title: Fixer Upper
programme.desc: A couple narrow down their search to three homes that would work for their family of five, and task Chip and Jo with determining which house has the most renovation potential for their budget.

programme.title: Storied Spaces
programme.desc: Within the worn halls of an 1890s Victorian, a modernized Italian monastery, and an American Revolutionary era stone house, families around the world take the once-in-a-lifetime opportunity to revive.

programme.title: Storied Spaces
programme.desc: From the shores of the Oregon Coast to the Washington wilderness and all the way to a Texas lake, visionary homeowners turn their home design dreams into reality with stunningly unique waterfront havens.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: A family of four is building a luxury glamping escape overlooking the Texas Hill Country, but the build process is anything but glamorous; with the rain, challenging terrain and critters, they must work together to get to the finish line.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: A young family in Alabama breaks ground on its forever home during peak hurricane season; between the delays from flooding, waiting for their building kit to arrive and falling glass, it will take everything they have to make this dream a reality.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: A Georgia couple is ready to tackle their next off-grid build: the ultimate watchtower guest house in the Blue Ridge Mountains; building 30 feet off the ground presents its challenges.

programme.title: Building Off the Grid
programme.desc: Two renovators purchase land in Colorado to build a new home away from home; they decide to use a rammed-earth process to construct all-natural walls; when winter comes early, they realize this process is more laborious than anticipated.
